content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of length NNN consisting of 111 and −1-1−1 only.

In one operation, Chef can choose any index iii (1≤i≤N)(1\le i \le N)(1≤i≤N) and multiply the element AiA_iAi​ by −1-1−1.

Find the **minimum** number of operations required to make the sum of the array equal to 000. Output `-1` if the sum of the array cannot be made 000.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
First line of each test case consists of a single integer NNN denoting the length of the array.
Second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum number of operations to make the sum of the array equal to 000. Output `-1` if it is not possible to make the sum equal to 000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The minimum number of operations required is 222. In the first operation, change A3A_3A3​ from 111 to −1-1−1. Similarly, in the second operation, change A4A_4A4​ from 111 to −1-1−1. Thus, the sum of the final array is 1+1−1−1=01+1-1-1=01+1−1−1=0.
Test case 222: It can be proven that the sum of the array cannot be made equal to zero by making any number of operations.
Test case 333: We can change A1A_1A1​ from 111 to −1-1−1 in one operation. Thus, the sum of the array becomes −1−1−1+1+1+1=0-1-1-1+1+1+1=0−1−1−1+1+1+1=0.
Test case 444: The sum of the array is already zero. Thus we do not need to make any operations.
